stem: A medical device testing study measures blood glucose measurements for 20 samples to determine if the process variability is within ±15% of the reference method. The sample standard deviation is 4.82 mg/dL (sample variance = 23.22). Estimate the true population standard deviation of blood glucose measurements and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Sample SD = 4.82 mg/dL. 95% CI for σ (chi-squared): [3.66, 7.04]. Point estimate: 4.82 mg/dL.

common_failure_modes: Using z-based CI for the mean instead of χ²-based CI for the variance
Reporting the sample SD (4.82) as the population SD without uncertainty
Not recognizing that confidence intervals for variance are asymmetric
